content:
bacek opened PR #12763 from bacek:bacek/fix-10252 to bytecodealliance:main:

Part of #10252.

I was hitting todo!() when was playing with ClickHouse WASM UDF, which caused whole ClickHouse to crash. It was only happening with Debug WASM builds and assert firing all over the place.

Implement the C embedder API for WebAssembly exception tags, resolving the long-standing todo!() in CExternType::new() that caused a Rust panic whenever a module with tag exports had its exports enumerated via the C API (e.g. wasm_module_exports / wasmtime_module_exports).

Changes:

  • Add wasm_tagtype_t type with wasm_tagtype_new/params/delete/copy and the standard as_externtype / externtype_as_tagtype cast functions
  • Add WASM_EXTERN_TAG = 4 constant to wasm_externkind_enum in wasm.h
  • Add CExternType::Tag(CTagType) variant; wire into CExternType::new() and wasm_externtype_kind()
  • Add wasmtime/types/tag.hh C++ wrapper (TagType / TagType::Ref)
  • Extend ExternType::Ref variant and ref_from_c() switch in extern.hh
  • Add TagType tests: construction, module export enumeration (regression for #10252), and wasm_externtype_kind / cast function correctness
bacek requested wasmtime-core-reviewers for a review on PR #12763.
bacek requested alexcrichton for a review on PR #12763.

alexcrichton commented on PR #12763:

Thanks! There's a number of things that CI is turning up here, but one of the more substantive things is that while wasm.h is vendored in this repository it's not the source of truth, we acquire it from elsewhere. Effectively we cannot change wasm.h, so modifications to that will need to be reverted here.

To handle that, though, it's fine to add functions to a wasmtime-specific header (e.g. a new tag.h or something like that). The functions can have a wasmtime_* specific constant/function associated with them then.
bacek updated PR #12763.

bacek commented on PR #12763:

Thanks! There's a number of things that CI is turning up here, but one of the more substantive things is that while wasm.h is vendored in this repository it's not the source of truth, we acquire it from elsewhere. Effectively we cannot change wasm.h, so modifications to that will need to be reverted here.

Where is wasm.h coming from?

To handle that, though, it's fine to add functions to a wasmtime-specific header (e.g. a new tag.h or something like that). The functions can have a wasmtime_* specific constant/function associated with them then.

Updated.
bacek updated PR #12763.

alexcrichton commented on PR #12763:

The script we use for vendoring is here.

For Wasmtime-specific functions, can you use the wasmtime_* prefix instead of wasm_*? We don't want to clash with any possible future additions to wasm.h. Additionally since we control the signatures we can make the internal implementations easier to work with. For example the constructor can take an engine argument, and instead of returning a list of types this could do something similar to component type accessors (also Wasmtime-specific) and return one-at-a-time through an iterator style pattern.
bacek updated PR #12763.
bacek updated PR #12763.

alexcrichton created PR review comment:

This is a bit subtle and will need to change. The definitions in this file of WASMTIME_EXTERN_* are discriminants for the wasmtime_extern_t structure below. Eventually that'll want a tag variant as well, in which case this WASMTIME_EXTERN_TAG subtly would serve a double-meaning.

To resolve this I'd recommend moving this #define into tag.h and renaming it perhaps to WASMTIME_EXTERNTYPE_TAG to make it clear that it's for the wasm_externtype_t discriminant rather than wasmtime_extern_t.

alexcrichton created PR review comment:

Here tag parameters are going to want to be communicated with their full value, not just their kind. Similar to above though I'd recommend following the Rust API here which is to just return a function type. This could return either a const wasm_functype_t* which is owned by the tag, or it's probably easier to return an owned wasm_functype_t* which the caller must deallocate.

alexcrichton created PR review comment:

As a further note on this, there's no need to leave room for the SHAREDMEMORY type here. This is ok to have value 4 since it's the next-in-sequence for the wasm_externkind_t union.
alexcrichton submitted PR review.

alexcrichton created PR review comment:

For the signature of this function I'd recommend following the same rough signature of Wasmtime itself. Namely in Wasmtime a tag is constructed from a full FuncType, whereas here all the pieces are provided.

In the original exception-handling proposal tags could only have parameters, but in the stack switching proposal this was relaxed to additionally allow result types too. Given that, and looking forward, I think we'll want to support constructing tags both with and without params/results, so at the very least this constructor needs to grow parameters to communicate the tag's result types. At that point I think it's probably easiest to just take a function type in the constructor.
github-actions[bot] added the label wasmtime:c-api on PR #12763.
bacek updated PR #12763.

bacek commented on PR #12763:

Erm. How I can re-run test which looks like some transient DNS error?

error: downloading 'https://github.com/google/googletest/archive/refs/tags/release-1.12.1.zip' failed

alexcrichton submitted PR review:

Thanks for this!
alexcrichton added PR #12763 c-api: implement wasm_tagtype_t for exception tag types (#10252) to the merge queue
alexcrichton merged PR #12763.
alexcrichton removed PR #12763 c-api: implement wasm_tagtype_t for exception tag types (#10252) from the merge queue
